question.text: Retirement planning should ideally begin at which stage of an individual's lifecycle?

answer.text: Pre-retirement stage when income is highest
Middle earning years when expenses stabilize
As early as the beginning of the working years
After completing all other financial goals

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Retirement planning should begin as early as possible — ideally from the beginning of working years. This maximizes the compounding period and reduces the monthly contribution needed. **The Trap:** Option A is too late — starting at the pre-retirement stage means lost decades of compounding. Option D is impractical since retirement cannot wait for other goals. **Cross-Exam Context:** Early start to financial planning is emphasized across Series V-A (SIP from young age), X-A (lifecycle financial planning), and XVII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even small amounts invested early make a huge difference. Rs.1,000/month from age 25 beats Rs.5,000/month from age 40 in many scenarios due to compounding.
